about: The consumer's git-tracked, human-authored demand ledger — phrases the app needs that the GOLD catalog does not have yet. `scripts/register_demand.py` PUSHES these into the shared catalog's `requests` table (assets.db), where the curator reads them and replies via `fulfilled_by`. This JSON is the authored source; the DB row is the shared interop surface, written only by that tool (single writer per column → no drift). See docs/audio-clip-contract.md §8 and docs/handoff-demand-tables.md.
invariants: This file lists ONLY open requests. Presence here == an open row in assets.db `requests`.

Fulfillment is migration, not a flag. The producer sets `requests.fulfilled_by` on promotion; the consumer then wires the asset_id into clips/clips.manifest.json, adds a `usage` row, and DELETES the request from here (which deletes the DB row on the next register-demand). 'Done' == 'gone'.

In-use dependencies are NOT authored here. clips/clips.manifest.json is the authoritative list of asset_ids we ship; register_demand.py mirrors it into the `usage` table for the 'don't de-curate these' signal.
Run `make register-demand` to push this ledger into assets.db and read back fulfilled_by (OPEN / READY / STALE).

requests.phrase: Up the water spout it goes!
requests.norm: up the water spout it goes
used_in: causality/7.x — the spider climbs the spout (beat 1 of 3)

constraints.voice: warm nursery-rhyme reading; the three spout beats should share one voice

requests.note: Full-phrase replacement for the single-word 'Up!'. The three itsy-bitsy-spider beats went back to full voiced phrases (the single words were too choppy). Curate these three together so they sound like one rhyme.

used_in: causality/7.x — rain falls on the spider (beat 2 of 3)

constraints.voice: same voice as the other two spout beats

requests.note: Replaces the single-word 'Down!' (was gold asset_380f5856d810 — that single word is now retired from use; this full phrase supersedes it).

used_in: causality/7.x — the spider washes out (beat 3 of 3)

constraints.voice: same voice as the other two spout beats

requests.note: Replaces the single-word 'Out!' (was gold asset_d703e812fbff — retired from use in favour of this full phrase).

used_in: mini-games/Picture Puzzle — each picture built, and the all-three finale
requests.note: Second-person 'You did it!' for the Picture Puzzle win. If no good 'you did it' recording exists, fulfil with the 'we did it' take — silver clip_ed372817a473 = gold asset_98cf57f963af (I already ship it as we_did_it.mp3). Producer's call. Once fulfilled I add 'you did it' → you_did_it.mp3 to CLIP_MAP and the existing wording upgrades from TTS to the clip with no reword.
